description: Atoms are the building blocks of skills. Each atom represents a single effect that the engine interprets. A skill can have multiple atoms executed in sequence.

move.description: Applies movement to the caster. Sub-kinds: directional (walk), jump, dash.

camera.description: Rotates the camera. Axis: yaw or pitch.

spawn_object.description: Instantiates a reusable scene asset at a position relative to the caster. The object is a trigger (Area3D) — it detects overlaps but has no physics response.

params.asset: Path to the .tscn asset to instantiate.
params.offset: Position relative to the caster's facing direction (forward, right, up).
params.dimensions: Scale of the spawned object relative to the asset's base size.
params.duration: How long the object lives before being freed (seconds). If omitted, defaults to 0.5s.
params.spawn_time: Total lifetime of the spawned object in seconds. If omitted, defaults to 0.5s. If present, the object persists for this duration.

movement.description: If present, the spawned object moves during its lifetime. Only processed if spawn_time is also defined.

params.direction: Movement direction. Special values: 'camera' = player camera forward, 'caster' = caster forward, 'target' = toward locked target. Or explicit Vector3.
params.speed: Movement speed in meters per second (m/s).

defaults.note: If spawn_time or duration are omitted from atom params, the engine uses 0.5s as default. Skills that want shorter/longer lifetime must explicitly declare spawn_time.

push.description: Applies a velocity impulse to all bodies overlapping a trigger. Direction is the caster's forward. Force is calculated from skill speed and caster weight.

params.force: Base push force (speed of the skill).
params.weight_multiplier: If true, force is multiplied by the caster's weight attribute.

hit.description: Composite atom: spawn_object + push. Spawns a trigger hitbox and pushes anything inside it. This is a convenience atom that combines spawn_object and push into one.

trigger.description: A trigger is an Area3D node that detects overlaps with physics bodies but does not push them. Used for hit detection, zones, and area effects.
trigger.implementation: Godot: Area3D with collision_layer=0, collision_mask set to target layers. On body_entered → apply effect.
trigger.reusable_assets: Trigger objects (hitbox_cube.tscn, hitbox_sphere.tscn) are stored in res://assets/ and reused across skills. Only dimensions and spawn offset change per skill.

movement_rules.description: Spawned objects can move during their lifetime. This enables projectiles, moving hitboxes, and directional attacks.
movement_rules.activation: Movement is ONLY processed if the atom params include BOTH 'spawn_time' AND 'movement'. If either is missing, the object is static (spawns at offset, stays there until freed).

direction_resolution.camera: Direction = player camera's forward vector (full 3D including pitch).
direction_resolution.caster: Direction = caster's forward vector (horizontal plane only).
direction_resolution.target: Direction = normalized vector from spawn position to locked target center.
direction_resolution.explicit: Direction = explicit Vector3 value {x, y, z} in world space.

movement_rules.speed_units: Speed is in meters per second (m/s). The object moves speed * delta each physics frame.
movement_rules.example: A projectile with spawn_time=2.0 and movement.speed=1.0 travels 2 meters total before being freed.
